news.summary: Nearly 38 million aesthetic procedures were conducted in 2024. Recently, in Mount Royal, N.J., on June 20, 2025, the International Society of Aesthetic Plastic Surgery (ISAPS) unveiled the findings from its yearly Global Survey on Aesthetic/Cosmetic Procedures during the ISAPS Olympiad World Congress.
news.headline: Global aesthetic report reveals rise in facial surgery trend, with eyelid surgery as top choice. Nearly 38 million procedures in 2024.

news.summary: According to recent reports, Apple is considering the implementation of generative artificial intelligence technology in order to enhance the efficiency of chip design processes. This move builds upon the company's previous years of utilizing AI-driven workflows. Apple aims to collaborate with essential Electronic Design Automation (EDA) partners to advance this initiative.
news.headline: Apple is considering using generative AI to accelerate custom chip design. The tech giant could leverage this technology to enhance efficiency with the assistance of key EDA partners, as reported by Reuters.

news.summary: In NEUILLY-SUR-SEINE, France, the Combined Shareholders’ Meeting of Bureau Veritas was presided over by Mr. Laurent Mignon, the Chairman of the Board of directors. The meeting, held at the company’s headquarters, saw the adoption of all resolutions by a significant majority. This included the approval of the statutory and consolidated financial statements for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2024. Additionally, a dividend distribution of €0.90 was approved.
news.headline: Bureau Veritas had a successful shareholders' meeting on June 19, 2025; all resolutions were adopted, including a €0.90 dividend.

news.summary: Shareholders of AS LHV Varahaldus, AS LHV Kindlustus, and AS LHV Finance, all part of AS LHV Group, plan to appoint Mihkel Torim to the Supervisory Board and as the Chairman of the Management Board of AS LHV Group starting July 22, 2025. The appointment of Torim to the Supervisory Board is for a term of up to five years. Additionally, the Financial Supervision Authority will review Torim's suitability for the role as a new Member of the Supervisory Board.
news.headline: Mihkel Torim to join LHV Varahaldus, LHV Kindlustus, and LHV Finance as new Supervisory Board Member starting from 22 July 2025, also to be Chairman of LHV Group.

news.summary: Significant progress has been made in the approval process based on pivotal study results, indicating enhancements in sustained disease remission and decreases in itch and oral corticosteroid usage compared to a placebo in adults with bullous pemphigoid. These findings are crucial as they demonstrate a positive outcome for individuals dealing with this condition, with the potential to significantly impact their quality of life. Such results showcase a promising development in the field of dermatology and may offer new treatment options for those affected by bullous pemphigoid.
news.headline: Dupixent approved in U.S. as first targeted medicine for Bullous Pemphigoid. Results show sustained remission and decrease in itch and corticosteroid use.

news.summary: A recent report revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has risen to 6.2%, marking an increase of 0.4% compared to the previous month. This increase translates to approximately 150,000 more people without jobs. The report also highlighted a decrease in job openings by 2.3%, indicating a challenging job market. Additionally, the average hourly wage saw a slight increase of 1.5%, offering some solace amidst the concerning unemployment figures. Experts suggest that the government needs to implement strategic measures to boost job creation and address the escalating unemployment crisis.
news.headline: New approval in the US for Dupixent - the sole targeted medication for bullous pemphigoid patients.

news.summary: Simply Solventless Concentrates Ltd. has released its Q1 2025 financial and operating results. The company reported record quarterly gross revenue of $12.4 million, EBITDA of $9.5 million, net and comprehensive income of $8.4 million, and adjusted EBITDA of $3.2 million. These results translate to annualized gross revenue of $49.6 million and annualized adjusted EBITDA of $12.8 million. The company's asset base grew from $10.9 million in Q1 2024 to $57.8 million in Q1 2025. The CEO highlighted the strong quarter, including acquisitions, offerings, and debt management. Key percentage increases in Q1 2025 compared to Q1 2024 included a 298% increase in gross revenue, 330% increase in net revenue, and 1,451% increase in EBITDA. The company aims to maximize profitability, cash flow, and balance sheet strength in 2025.
news.headline: Simply Solventless reported Q1 2025 results: $49.6M gross revenue ($0.459/share) and $12.8M adjusted EBITDA ($0.120/share).

news.summary: In Clermont-Ferrand, France, on June 20, 2025, CARBIOS (Euronext Growth Paris: ALCRB) disclosed the outcomes of its Ordinary and Extraordinary general Meeting held on June 19, 2025. CARBIOS is a trailblazer in advancing and commercializing biological technologies aimed at revolutionizing the plastic and textiles life cycle.
news.headline: Carbios reveals AGM results on June 19, 2025, and their Board composition; pioneering in plastic and textiles.

news.summary: On June 20, 2025, in Shanghai, LONGi, a renowned company specializing in PV technology innovation, sealed a strategic partnership with TÜV Rheinland, a global independent organization specializing in testing, inspection, and certification services. This collaboration was announced during the 2025 SNEC exhibition in Shanghai, emphasizing their commitment to advancing solar technology. LONGi, a key player in the industry, aims to capitalize on this alliance to further enhance their position in the market. The agreement signifies a significant milestone for both companies, as they look forward to utilizing their expertise to drive innovation and ensure quality standards.
news.headline: LONGi, a leader in PV tech, signs deal with TÜV Rheinland for quality upgrade in PV industry at SNEC exhibition in Shanghai.

news.headline: Arizona Senate approved a bill to create a state digital asset fund from seized crypto: now moving to House for discussion.

news.summary: In Calgary, AB, on June 20, 2025, Strathcona Resources Ltd. addressed the recent Directors' Circular issued by MEG Energy Corp. on June 16, 2025. The circular from MEG's board of directors was in response to Strathcona's Offer. The Board of Directors of MEG provided their response to the Offer in the circular.
news.headline: Strathcona supports MEG's strategic alternatives process in response to the Directors' Circular filed on June 16, 2025.

news.summary: A company linked to former President Donald Trump, DT Marks DeFi LLC, has recently decreased its ownership in World Liberty Financial, a cryptocurrency venture endorsed by the U.S. presidential family. The reduction in stake involves DT Marks DeFi holding a 40% equity share in the firm behind World Liberty Financial, indicating a decline of 20 percentage points from its previous ownership of 60%. Reports revealed that this stake adjustment was done discreetly, with Forbes stating that as of December, DT Marks DeFi possessed a 75% interest in World Liberty Financial based on financial disclosures. Furthermore, the company holds the governance token of World Liberty Financial, known as WLFI, and is entitled to receive 75% of... (The full story can be found on Benzinga.com)
news.headline: Trump-linked company DT Marks DeFi LLC reduces stake in World Liberty Financial from 60% to 40%, per Forbes report.

news.summary: The Arizona Senate has passed a bill, known as House Bill 2324, aimed at establishing a Bitcoin reserve. This legislation, which had previously been unsuccessful in the House, has now been approved by the Senate.
news.headline: Arizona Senate resurrects Bitcoin reserve bill (HB 2324) after reconsideration vote following its failure in the House.

news.summary: Bitcoin remained stable on Thursday, but its cheaper counterparts grabbed the market's attention. Bitcoin Cash surged by more than 7%, making it the third-largest gainer in the past 24 hours. Its trading volume spiked by 77% to reach $733 million, pointing to significant liquidity and trader engagement. This recent increase pushed BCH's weekly gains to 23%. Furthermore, Bitcoin SV also rose by 4.40% within the same period, resulting in a weekly return of 8.32%. These fluctuations indicate the ongoing activity and interest in the cryptocurrency market. For more details, visit the story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bitcoin Cash spiked over 7% with $733 million in trading volume, while Bitcoin SV rose 4.40% in 24 hours.

news.summary: According to cybersecurity company Cisco Talos, threat actors from North Korea have been using malicious software to target professionals in the blockchain industry. These cybercriminals are deploying malware through counterfeit cryptocurrency job websites in an attempt to steal wallet credentials. This poses a significant risk to individuals who work in blockchain, as their personal information and digital assets could be compromised. It is essential for blockchain professionals to remain vigilant and take necessary precautions to protect themselves against these cyber threats.
news.headline: North Korea using new malware to target crypto workers, stealing wallet credentials, says Cisco Talos.
